rationale_for_baseline_tier: A patient who can swallow water and saliva with a normal voice and no drooling, afebrile with no glandular swelling, has a xerostomia friction-dysphagia flare — low acuity, managed with lubrication, dietary change and routine rheumatology review. The only escalations are for a true food-bolus impaction/airway compromise or suppurative sialadenitis, both absent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Inability to swallow your own saliva, drooling, a voice change, or noisy breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests a true food-bolus impaction/airway compromise.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't swallow your own saliva, start drooling, your voice changes, or your breathing gets noisy, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever with painful swelling in front of the ear or under the jaw
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a fever with painful swelling in front of your ear or under your jaw, see a doctor the same day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise ongoing lubrication to prevent recurrence and monitor for oral candidiasis.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Nothing is blocking your throat — you can swallow water and saliva and your voice is normal. The burning is severe dryness from your Sjogren's. Use artificial saliva before meals, avoid dry/crumbly foods, sip water with food, and see your GP/rheumatologist about a saliva-stimulating medicine. Go to ED if you can't swallow your saliva, start drooling, your voice changes, or you get a fever with painful jaw/ear swelling.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP/rheumatology within 2-3 weeks; ED for bolus/airway features; same-day for parotitis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless a true bolus or glandular infection develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a xerostomia friction-dysphagia flare in Sjogren's; food-bolus impaction and suppurative sialadenitis excluded (swallows water/saliva, normal voice, afebrile); artificial saliva/dietary advice given; secretagogue review recommended; bolus/parotitis safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without screening for a true bolus/airway compromise or sialadenitis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A genuine food-bolus impaction is missed and progresses to aspiration/complete obstruction.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for friction dysphagia with an intact swallow and normal voice is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the oropharynx/glands or endoscope remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use the ability to swallow water/saliva and normal voice to exclude a true bolus, advise lubrication, and give a bolus/parotitis safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either over-triage as a food-bolus obstruction or reassure without screening bolus/airway features.
